steps: Create a PAT (or OAuth app token) with `manage_billing:copilot` OR `read:org` / `read:enterprise`.
Sent as Authorization: Bearer (with Accept: application/vnd.github+json + X-GitHub-Api-Version).

data.pii_posture: PII-free: the aggregate metrics object carries no per-developer identity (one emission per day). The newer per-user NDJSON usage-metrics report is deferred behind a future redaction-and-pass model.

copilot.description: GitHub Copilot org metrics (aggregate, PII-free) as governed evidence.

instructions.text: Create a GitHub PAT/OAuth token with manage_billing:copilot or read:org.
Paste the token (sent as Authorization: Bearer).
Provide the org-templated metrics endpoint (.../orgs/<org>/copilot/metrics).
Test the connection: an active poll returns aggregate daily metrics (one emission per day), no per-developer identity.

copilot.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Aggregate PII-free parse surface + the live fetch-half (build_copilot_spec) are built and harness-proven against a recorded fixture (contract verified 2026-06-08). Gated on operator review + a live poll with a real token. To flip: provide a read:org token + the org-templated endpoint; wire GatewaySink; run the live poll; review before promoting.
copilot.name: GitHub Copilot

runtime_config.description: GET .../orgs/<org>/copilot/metrics — template YOUR org in (default carries an ORG placeholder).
runtime_config.label: Copilot metrics endpoint (org-templated)

runtime_config.description: GitHub REST API version header (valid; EOL 2028-03-10).
runtime_config.label: X-GitHub-Api-Version

runtime_config.description: Results per page (max 100; page-number pagination, 100-day lookback).
runtime_config.label: Page size

wire_gates: Contract verified docs.github.com 2026-06-08: top-level JSON array of day objects (one emission/day); page/per_page pagination (max 100; 100-day lookback) via PageNumberPager (stop-on-short-page). X-GitHub-Api-Version 2022-11-28 is an argument, not baked.
Operator templates their org into base_url (required; the default carries an ORG placeholder that will 404).
Live flip gated on operator human review + a live poll with a real read:org token (ADR-0012).

credentials.header: Authorization: Basic (API key as username, empty password)
credentials.label: Team Admin API key

steps: A team administrator mints an Admin API key in the Cursor dashboard (team settings).
The key is sent as HTTP Basic with the key as the username and an empty password.

data.pii_posture: PII-free by construction: parse-time allowlist reads only numeric metrics + mostUsedModel + the OPAQUE integer userId; email/name/clientVersion are NEVER read (FX-SEC-001 does not detect a generic email, so the control is the allowlist, not a downstream screen). Free-text day/model pass through redact().
data.redaction: parse-time PII exclusion (allowlist) + redact() on free-text; opaque userId for pseudonymous per-developer attribution (SG-2026-06-05-D)

cursor.description: Cursor team daily-usage metrics (Admin API) as PII-free, governed evidence.

instructions.text: A team admin mints a Cursor Admin API key (team settings).
Paste the Admin API key (sent as HTTP Basic username, empty password).
Provide the date-range request body {startDate, endDate} in epoch milliseconds (range <= 30 days).
Test the connection: an active poll returns PII-free per-user-day usage rows (opaque userId only).

cursor.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Parse surface (PII-free allowlist) + the live fetch-half (build_cursor_spec) are built and harness-proven against a recorded fixture; contract re-verified live 2026-06-11. Gated on operator human review + a live poll with a real team Admin API key. To flip: provide the key + a date-range body; wire GatewaySink; run the live poll; review before promoting.
cursor.name: Cursor

runtime_config.description: The Cursor Admin API daily-usage endpoint (host pinned api.cursor.com; verified 2026-06-11).
runtime_config.label: Daily-usage endpoint

runtime_config.description: POST body {startDate, endDate} in epoch MILLISECONDS, range <= 30 days. page/pageSize (body) paginate; the reference client fetches one page (widen pageSize for teams > one page).
runtime_config.label: Date-range request body

wire_gates: Contract verified live 2026-06-11: POST https://api.cursor.com/teams/daily-usage-data, HTTP Basic (key as username), body {startDate,endDate} epoch-ms <= 30 days, `data` envelope; rows carry userId/email/metrics, no `name`.

Pagination is POST-body `page`/`pageSize` with response `pagination.hasNextPage` (verified 2026-06-11). The reference client (build_cursor_spec) issues a SINGLE request and is NOT yet wired for multi-page — a team larger than one page truncates; widen `pageSize` or wire a body-page pager before relying on full coverage. (runtime/poll_specs.py build_cursor_spec carries stale 'inferred/unverified' comments to reconcile.)
PII control is the parse-time allowlist (no downstream screen for generic email) — do NOT relax it. Operator runs the live poll + reviews before promoting to Live (ADR-0012).

steps: In your Devin organization settings, create a Service-User API key (cog_ prefix). It is shown once at creation and is RBAC-scoped; a Personal Access Token works too where available.
Copy the key — it is sent as an Authorization: Bearer header. Devin publishes no webhooks, so this is the only credential (active poll only).

data.pii_posture: Session free-text (title, structured_output, message trail) may carry secrets/PII, so it is passed through adapter.core.redaction.redact (redact-and-pass) before emit; the first pull_requests[].pr_url is kept un-redacted as the artifact location (github/gitlab/jira precedent). FX-SEC-001 producer screen is the un-bypassable backstop.
data.redaction: redact-and-pass (free-text scrubbed of secret/PHI/PAN + email/phone; FX-SEC-001 is the hard backstop)

devin.description: Devin agentic-coding sessions (v3 enterprise API) as redacted, governed evidence.

instructions.text: Create a Service-User API key (cog_) in your Devin organization settings.
Paste the Service-User API key (sent as Authorization: Bearer <key>).
Provide the v3 sessions base URL with your org id templated in: https://api.devin.ai/v3/organizations/<org_id>/sessions.
Test the connection: an active poll should return recent sessions as redacted evidence (one emission per session).

devin.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Parse + redaction surface and the live fetch-half (build_devin_spec, runner-wired) are code-complete and harness-proven against a reference sink — but still incomplete: the Live flip is gated on operator human review + a live poll against the real Devin v3 enterprise API with a real Service-User (cog_) key. To flip: provide the api key + the org-templated base_url; wire GatewaySink; run the live poll; review before promoting to Live.
devin.name: Devin

runtime_config.description: The v3 sessions list endpoint with your org templated in: https://api.devin.ai/v3/organizations/<org_id>/sessions. Consumed by runtime build_devin_spec(base_url=...); there is no default (the operator supplies org_id).
runtime_config.label: Sessions endpoint base URL

wire_gates: The operator templates org_id into the REQUIRED base_url (https://api.devin.ai/v3/organizations/<org_id>/sessions); build_devin_spec has no default base_url, so a missing value fails closed (ConfigError) before any request.

v3 contract (items envelope; pull_requests[] of {pr_url, pr_state}; cursor end_cursor/has_next_page re-sent as ?after=; Bearer cog_ Service-User key) was doc-verified 2026-06-08 and re-verified live 2026-06-11 (docs.devinenterprise.com) — but UNVERIFIED against the live API with a real cog_ key. The operator must run the live poll and review the result before promoting to Live (ADR-0012).

This connector targets the v3 ENTERPRISE API (docs.devinenterprise.com). The parallel v1 API (docs.devin.ai: GET /v1/sessions, `sessions` envelope, singular `pull_request`, limit/offset, apk_ keys) is the 2026-06-08-corrected drift shape — do NOT cite or wire it (SG-2026-06-11-A).

credentials.header: X-Hub-Signature-256 (sha256= hex HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body)
credentials.label: Webhook signing secret

steps: When you create the repository (or org) webhook, set a Secret.
Copy it — Bicameral verifies X-Hub-Signature-256 (hex HMAC-SHA256, sha256= prefix) over the raw body, constant-time, fail-closed.

data.pii_posture: PR title + body are free-text -> passed through adapter.core.redaction.redact (redact-and-pass: scrubs secret/PHI/PAN + email/phone). author is the PUBLIC PR-author login (the artifact author, like the kept pr_url precedent), not redacted. FX-SEC-001 is the un-bypassable backstop. X-GitHub-Delivery dedups replays.
data.redaction: redact-and-pass (PR body + title); public author login surfaced

github.description: GitHub pull-request webhook events as redact-and-pass governed evidence.

instructions.text: Create a GitHub webhook on Pull request events, pointing at your Bicameral receiver URL.
Paste the webhook signing secret you set when creating the webhook.
Test the connection: a signed test pull_request delivery should produce one emission (body redact-and-passed); a replayed delivery is deduped.

github.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Parse + X-Hub-Signature-256 verify + dedup are built and harness-proven; PR body/title redact-and-passed. Gated on operator human review + a live signed delivery with a real webhook secret + a provisioned receiver. To flip: create the webhook + secret + receiver; wire GatewaySink; send a signed test delivery; review before promoting.
github.name: GitHub

steps: In the GitHub repo/org settings, create a webhook subscribed to Pull request events (content type application/json).
Set its Payload URL to your Bicameral webhook receiver (see receiver below).

webhook.signature_scheme: hex HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body (sha256= prefix)

wire_gates: Webhook path is built + verified: X-Hub-Signature-256 (sha256= hex HMAC-SHA256, constant-time, fail-closed) + X-GitHub-Delivery dedup + envelope unwrap (top-level number injected into the nested pull_request). PR body/title redact-and-passed.
The ACTIVE REST fetch (GET pulls) is in the connector's capabilities but is NOT wired in the runtime this cycle -- webhook is the ingest path; an active poller is a future enhancement.

Webhook RECEIPT is operator-runtime: provision the receiver URL + inject github_webhook into the receive path (the CLI runs active fetches, not webhook receipt). Live flip gated on operator review + a signed live delivery (ADR-0012).

steps: An access token is SHORT-LIVED (~1 hour; honor the returned expires_in) — pasting one is a quick test, not a durable setup.

Durable option A (user OAuth): register an OAuth client (Testing publishing status = NO verification for your own/test users), grant the documents.readonly / drive.readonly scopes, and persist the REFRESH token + client id/secret. The stdlib runtime.RefreshTokenSecretResolver mints fresh access tokens from these (plain POST, no RSA).

Durable option B (server, service account): use a service-account JSON; share the doc with the SA email (or use domain-wide delegation to impersonate). This needs RS256 JWT signing (google-auth) and is OPERATOR-RUNTIME — NOT stdlib.

Quick test token: the OAuth 2.0 Playground (developers.google.com/oauthplayground) issues a ~1h access token for the selected scopes. Restricted-scope verification only applies to apps DISTRIBUTED to other users.

data.pii_posture: Document title + flattened body text (untrusted, possibly PII-dense). FX-SEC-001 producer screen rejects secret/PHI/PAN before emit.

google_drive.description: Google Docs documents fetched by id (documents.get) as governed evidence.

instructions.text: Connect Google and grant read-only Docs/Drive access (documents.readonly / drive.readonly). Token refresh is handled by the operator runtime.
Provide the Google Doc id to fetch.
Test the connection: documents.get should return the document; its text is screened before emission.

google_drive.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Code-ready (FX-GDRIVE-002, documents.get) and harness-proven against a reference sink — but still incomplete: the Live flip is gated on operator human review + a live test against the real Google Docs API with a real OAuth credential. To flip: provide a durable credential (refresh token via RefreshTokenSecretResolver, or a service-account JSON at the operator runtime) + a document id; wire GatewaySink; run the live test; review before promoting to Live.
google_drive.name: Google Drive (Docs)

runtime_config.description: The Google Doc id to fetch (validated [A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,200}; spliced into GET /v1/documents/{id}).
runtime_config.label: Document ID

wire_gates: Bearer-header assumption + multi-tab docs confirmed against a live response before the Live flip. Multi-tab docs (content under tabs[].documentTab.body) emit title-only — extract_document_text walks the legacy body; includeTabsContent/tabs[] deferred.

OAuth token refresh is now stdlib (runtime.RefreshTokenSecretResolver, FX-RUNTIME-006) — pasting a raw access token is a ~1h TEST only; the durable path is the refresh resolver (user OAuth) or a service-account JSON (operator-runtime, RS256/google-auth, not stdlib). Folder polling (Drive files.list) + push-notification webhooks remain deferred.

Live flip GATED on human review + live testing: code-complete and harness-proven through runtime/ against a reference sink, but UNVERIFIED against the live Google Docs API with a real OAuth credential — no machine has minted a real token and fetched a real document yet. The operator must run the live test (durable path = the refresh resolver) and review the result before promoting; until then this connector is flip-ready, NOT Live (ADR-0012).

steps: Create a Granola API key (grn_ prefix) in your Granola workspace settings.
Sent as Authorization: Bearer grn_<key>.

data.pii_posture: Meeting content is PII-dense and the provider gives no redaction guidance. The transcript + title are passed through adapter.core.redaction.redact (redact-and-pass: scrubs secret/PHI/PAN + email/phone). The meeting OWNER's identity (owner.name/email) is NEVER surfaced (author dropped, PII-safe). speaker is an anonymized object (source/diarization label), never read. FX-SEC-001 is the un-bypassable backstop.

granola.description: Granola meeting notes + transcript (redact-and-pass) as governed evidence.

instructions.text: Create a Granola API key (grn_) in your Granola workspace settings.
Paste the Granola API key (sent as Authorization: Bearer grn_<key>).
Test the connection: an active poll returns recent notes; the transcript is redact-and-passed and the owner's identity is never surfaced.

granola.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Redacted transcript parse surface + the live fetch-half (build_granola_spec) are built and harness-proven against a recorded fixture; contract re-verified live 2026-06-11; the owner-vs-attendees drift + transcript-PII gap are fixed (L2). Gated on operator review + a live poll with a real grn_ key. To flip: provide the key; wire GatewaySink; run the live poll; review before promoting.
granola.name: Granola

runtime_config.description: The notes-with-transcript endpoint (verified 2026-06-11). The incremental created_after watermark + two-phase commit are operator-runtime.
runtime_config.label: Notes endpoint

wire_gates: Contract re-verified live 2026-06-11 (public-api.granola.ai): GET /v1/notes?include=transcript, Bearer grn_; `notes` envelope; cursor `cursor`/`hasMore` pagination; note carries owner{name,email}, title, summary, transcript[{speaker:{source,diarization_label},text}], created_at.
PII (SG-2026-06-11-D, fixed this cycle): identity is `owner` (NOT `attendees`); transcript + title are redact-and-passed; owner identity is dropped (FX-SEC-001 does not catch a generic name/email).
Live flip gated on operator human review + a live poll with a real grn_ key (ADR-0012).

credentials.header: X-Hub-Signature (sha256= hex HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body, WebSub)

steps: When you create the Jira webhook, set a Secret (classic webhooks sign with it).
Copy it — Bicameral verifies X-Hub-Signature (hex HMAC-SHA256, sha256= prefix) over the raw body, constant-time, fail-closed.

data.pii_posture: Title/excerpt come from issue.fields.summary (a string), passed through adapter.core.redaction.redact (redact-and-pass: scrubs secret/PHI/PAN + email/phone). fields.description is an Atlassian Document Format OBJECT and is NEVER read. The issue actor's displayName (a real name) is NOT surfaced (author dropped, PII-safe). FX-SEC-001 is the un-bypassable backstop.

Paste the webhook signing secret you set when creating the webhook.
Test the connection: a signed test issue event should produce one emission (summary redact-and-passed); a duplicate delivery is deduped.

jira.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Parse + X-Hub-Signature verify + best-effort dedup are built and harness-proven; summary redact-and-passed, ADF description never read, actor identity dropped. Gated on operator review + a live signed delivery with a real webhook secret + a provisioned receiver. To flip: create the webhook + secret + receiver; wire GatewaySink; send a signed test delivery; review before promoting.
jira.name: Jira

steps: In Jira (system or app webhooks), create a webhook on Issue created/updated/deleted with a Secret.
Set its URL to your Bicameral webhook receiver (see receiver below).

webhook.signature_scheme: hex HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body (X-Hub-Signature, sha256= prefix, WebSub)

wire_gates: Webhook path built + verified: X-Hub-Signature (sha256= hex HMAC-SHA256, constant-time, fail-closed). Jira documents NO anti-replay window, so best-effort dedup (X-Atlassian-Webhook-Identifier -> issue.id -> body-hash) is the only replay guard. summary redact-and-passed; ADF description never read.
The ACTIVE REST fetch is in capabilities but NOT wired this cycle; Connect-JWT / Forge / Automation auth are deferred -- the classic signed webhook is the ingest path.
Webhook RECEIPT is operator-runtime (receiver URL + jira_webhook injection). Live flip gated on operator review + a signed live delivery (ADR-0012).

steps: In your Linear workspace settings, create a personal API key.

linear.description: Linear issues — webhook events + active GraphQL fetch — as governed evidence.

Create a Linear webhook on Issue events, pointing at your Bicameral receiver URL.
Paste the webhook signing secret Linear shows when the webhook is created.
Test the connection: a signed test webhook should produce one emission; an active GraphQL fetch should return recent issues.

linear.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Code-ready on both paths (FX-LINEAR-002 webhook, FX-LINEAR-003 GraphQL) and harness-proven against a reference sink — but still incomplete: the Live flip is gated on operator human review + a live test against Linear's real API/webhook with real secrets. To flip: provide the API key, the webhook signing secret, and a webhook receiver URL; wire GatewaySink; run the live test; review before promoting to Live.
linear.name: Linear

runtime_config.description: Issues fetched per GraphQL page (Linear Relay cursor; default 50).
runtime_config.label: GraphQL page size

steps: In Linear, create a webhook subscribed to Issue events.
Set its URL to your Bicameral webhook receiver (see receiver below).

webhook.signature_scheme: hex HMAC-SHA256 over the raw request body

wire_gates: Two-secret model (RESOLVED, FX-RUNTIME-005): Linear needs two namespaced credential keys — 'linear' (GraphQL API key, modes: active) + 'linear_webhook' (webhook signing secret, modes: webhook). The resolver resolves each by key (env BICAMERAL_LINEAR / BICAMERAL_LINEAR_WEBHOOK), and assert_runnable is mode-scoped so an active `runtime.cli run linear` requires only 'linear'. Residual: injecting 'linear_webhook' into deliver_webhook's receive path stays operator-runtime (the CLI runs active fetches, not webhook receipt).
GraphQL issue field set (id/identifier/title/description/url/updatedAt/state.name) confirmed against a live response before the Live flip (verify-before-cite).

Live flip GATED on human review + live testing: code-complete and harness-proven through runtime/ against a reference sink, but UNVERIFIED against Linear's live API/webhook with real secrets — no machine has hit the live endpoint yet. The operator must run the live test and review the result before promoting; until then this connector is flip-ready, NOT Live (ADR-0012).

data.pii_posture: Public registry metadata only (server name/title/description, repository/website URL, version). PII-free; no credential; no per-person data.

instructions.text: No credential needed — this is a public, no-auth registry. Review the server-list API.
Test the connection: an active poll returns the public MCP server list (one emission per server).

mcp_registry.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Public no-auth parse + live fetch-half (build_mcp_registry_spec) built, harness-proven, and runner-wired this cycle. No secret to provision. To flip: configure the gateway endpoint; run the public poll; review before promoting to Live.

wire_gates: Verified contract (registry.modelcontextprotocol.io/openapi.yaml 2026-06-08): public no-auth GET /v0/servers; list under `servers`; each entry nests the server under `server`; cursor pagination via response `metadata.nextCursor` (no has-more — stop on absent).
No credential (public). Live flip is gated only on the operator wiring GatewaySink + running the public poll + review (ADR-0012).

steps: Create a dedicated integration user with `rest_service` + the incident table-read roles (least privilege).
The password is the resolved secret; the instance host + username are non-secret runtime config (below).

data.pii_posture: Incident description/short_description are free-text that routinely carry secrets/PII -> passed through adapter.core.redaction.redact (redact-and-pass: scrubs secret/PHI/PAN + email/phone). The caller_id/caller identity field is NEVER read. number/state/priority are the non-PII metadata surface. FX-SEC-001 is the un-bypassable backstop.

Paste the integration-user password (Basic auth).
Provide the instance host + integration username (non-secret runtime config).
Test the connection: an active poll returns recent incidents with description redact-and-passed; caller identity is never read.

Parse + redact-and-pass surface + the live fetch-half (build_servicenow_spec, Basic + offset pagination) are built and harness-proven against recorded 2-page fixtures; the SSRF-4 URL-injection gap is fixed (#133). servicenow.name: ServiceNow

runtime_config.description: Bare hostname, e.g. acme.service-now.com (host-validated before the URL is built; no path/query injection).
runtime_config.label: ServiceNow instance host

runtime_config.description: Non-secret Basic-auth username for the integration user (paired with the resolved password).
runtime_config.label: Integration-user username

runtime_config.description: Rows per page; sysparm_offset advances by this until a short page (offset pagination).
runtime_config.label: Page size (sysparm_limit)

runtime_config.description: Optional field selection (urlencoded; cannot inject sibling query params).
runtime_config.label: sysparm_fields (comma list)

wire_gates: Table API documented shape: GET https://<instance>/api/now/table/incident, HTTP Basic, `{result:[...]}` envelope, sysparm_offset/sysparm_limit offset pagination (stop-on-short-page). instance is host-validated + the URL is urllib-built (SSRF-4 fix, #133) so instance/fields cannot inject host/path/query.
OAuth 2.0 is a deferred alternative; Basic auth this cycle.
Live flip gated on operator human review + a live Table-API poll with a real integration-user credential (ADR-0012).

steps: In your Slack app's Basic Information, copy the Signing Secret.
Bicameral verifies X-Slack-Signature (v0= HMAC-SHA256 over 'v0:{ts}:{body}') with a 5-minute replay window, constant-time, fail-closed.

data.pii_posture: Message text is PII-dense human communication -> passed through adapter.core.redaction.redact (redact-and-pass: scrubs secret/PHI/PAN + email/phone). author is the OPAQUE Slack user id (e.g. U0123ABC) -- pseudonymous (the operator holds the id->identity mapping; SG-2026-06-05-D), not a name/email. FX-SEC-001 is the un-bypassable backstop.
data.redaction: redact-and-pass (message text); opaque Slack user id surfaced (pseudonymous)

slack.description: Slack message events as redact-and-pass governed evidence (read/ingest).

Paste the Slack app Signing Secret.
Test the connection: a signed message event should produce one emission (text redact-and-passed); the url_verification handshake + replays are dropped.

slack.live_readiness: Flip-ready, NOT yet Live. Parse + X-Slack-Signature verify (v0, 5-min replay) + handshake/dedup are built and harness-proven; message text redact-and-passed, opaque user id surfaced. Gated on operator review + a live signed event with a real signing secret + a provisioned receiver. To flip: create the app + signing secret + Request URL receiver; wire GatewaySink; send a signed test event; review before promoting.
slack.name: Slack

steps: Create a Slack app, enable Event Subscriptions, subscribe to message events.
Set the Request URL to your Bicameral webhook receiver (see receiver below); the url_verification handshake is handled.

webhook.signature_scheme: v0= hex HMAC-SHA256 over 'v0:{X-Slack-Request-Timestamp}:{raw_body}', 5-minute replay window

wire_gates: Webhook path built + verified: X-Slack-Signature v0= HMAC-SHA256 over 'v0:{ts}:{body}' + 5-minute replay window (constant-time, fail-closed); url_verification handshake + unverified deliveries normalize to []. Message text redact-and-passed; opaque user id (pseudonymous) surfaced.
Trust tier T2 (read/ingest). Notify/write (T3+) is deferred (ADR-0008, evidence-before-action).
Webhook RECEIPT is operator-runtime (receiver URL + slack_webhook injection). Live flip gated on operator review + a signed live event (ADR-0012).
